title: oxytropis sordida subsp. sordida
intro: Oxytropis sordida subsp. sordida, also known as the rough-leaved locoweed, is a type of flowering plant in the pea family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and to treat urinary tract infections. The health benefits of Oxytropis sordida subsp. sordida include its potential to help reduce inflammation, alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, and act as an anti-bacterial agent. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although it's essential to consult with a medical professional before using any herbal remedy.
